titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Carey to Alexander Mackintosh, 3 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Carey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Patrick Carey to plumber Alexander Finlay Mackintosh requesting repairs to the electric bell and gas.
Father William Behan was a parish priest in Cahirciveen, Co. Kerry. Formerly an army chaplain in the First World War, Fr. Behan returned to Kerry and took an active interest in the on-goings of the local IRA brigade. Behan collected signatures for a petition asserting that the local brigade was not doing enough.

msIdentifier.collection: Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians BG104/B/4

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Killarney Union,
Clerk's Office, 3rd Decr. 1915, Dear Sir, The Electric Bell here is out of
order. Please attend today to repair it.
The Gas in back Office also requires attention. Yours faithfully,
Clerk of Union. Mr. A.F.Mackintosh,
